task_completion.description: bcWebCam application is open and focused.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam application window is visible and appears as the active window on the screen.

task_completion.description: The VIN barcode image is displayed on the screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image of a Code 39 barcode is open and visible on the screen, positioned so bcWebCam can capture it.

task_completion.description: The VIN is successfully scanned and extracted by bcWebCam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam interface displays the decoded 17-character alphanumeric VIN (expected: {{expected_vin}}).

task_completion.description: The agent navigates to the Windows Desktop where the diagnostic report is located.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Windows Desktop or File Explorer showing the Desktop directory is visible, containing the file '{{original_filename}}'.

task_completion.description: The diagnostic report file is renamed to include the scanned VIN.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file on the Desktop is shown with the new name '{{expected_filename}}' (or 'diagnostic_<SCANNED_VIN>.pdf' matching the decoded VIN).

integrity.description: The file was renamed using the Windows GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file renaming action is visible in the Windows Desktop or File Explorer interface, not via a command prompt or PowerShell window.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual bcWebCam software to decode the barcode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The decoded VIN appears within the genuine bcWebCam application UI, rather than being manually typed into a text editor or extracted via a custom script.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste a hardcoded VIN without scanning.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen obtaining the VIN from the bcWebCam interface before renaming the file, rather than magically knowing the VIN without a successful scan.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected VIN is {{expected_vin}} and the expected final filename is {{expected_filename}}. Note: The task environment is known to be defective (missing the prerequisite barcode image 'vin_barcode.png' and setup scripts). The agent may be unable to find the barcode or the initial '{{original_filename}}' file. Score strictly based on the visual evidence present in the trajectory; if the starting state is missing and the agent cannot complete the steps, award points only for the steps successfully shown.
